critic_raw:
Three interconnected methodological problems.

First, Section 3 states: 'all preprocessing and feature engineering transformers are fit on the entire dataset before any data is split.' This is explicit preprocessing leakage. For sensor time-series, z-score normalization fit on global data mean and std means the test set's statistical distribution directly influences the normalization applied to all splits.

Second, the split uses 'stratified random sampling across all available months, holding out a random 15% of observations.' For continuous sensor data from manufacturing equipment, time-series observations are temporally dependent. Random sampling mixes future sensor readings into training windows. A machine with a known failure in month 55 has surrounding windows randomly distributed across train and test.

Third and most critically, the hypothesis claims the RNN 'reduces unplanned equipment downtime by 25%' but the evaluation metrics are F1-score, AUROC, precision, and recall—classification metrics on binary failure predictions, not operational downtime reduction metrics. To validate the 25% downtime claim, the experiment would need to simulate deployment with actual intervention counts and counterfactual downtime reduction.
all_issues_raised: Preprocessing transformers fit on entire dataset before splitting — explicit preprocessing leakage
Stratified random sampling on time-series sensor data creates temporal leakage between failure-adjacent windows
Hypothesis claims 25% downtime reduction but experiment measures only classification metrics — operational claim unsupported
SPC baseline compared to RNN on classification metrics — SPC is not a classifier in the same sense

round_transcripts.critic: Three problems: (1) Preprocessing fit on entire dataset — explicit per Section 3. (2) Stratified random sampling on sensor time-series mixes failure-adjacent windows across splits — temporal leakage. (3) The 25% downtime reduction hypothesis is evaluated only with classification metrics — operational claim entirely unsupported by the measurement design.
round_transcripts.defender:
On preprocessing: global sensor statistics (mean, std) characterize the facility's stable operating baseline. Fitting on the full dataset reduces estimation variance. Rolling features (1-hour, 24-hour) are computed from raw values before normalization and thus are not contaminated.

On temporal sampling: the primary concern for equipment failure prediction is different machine types with different failure rates—temporal ordering within a machine is less critical than balanced machine-type representation.

On downtime reduction: the 25% figure is a target framing based on industry benchmarks for predictive maintenance programs. The experiment tests prediction accuracy—operational deployment simulation is a follow-on study.

round_transcripts.critic:
On preprocessing: Section 3 says 'all preprocessing and feature engineering transformers are fit on the entire dataset.' 'Feature engineering transformers' explicitly includes rolling statistics construction and normalization. The leakage encompasses these derived features.

On temporal sampling: machine degradation is a cumulative process—a machine's sensor patterns at month 34-36 are qualitatively different from month 1-3. Within-machine temporal contamination is a real concern.

On downtime reduction: the defender concedes this is 'target framing, not a claim being directly tested.' This confirms the stated hypothesis is not validated by the experimental design.
round_transcripts.defender:
I concede on downtime reduction: the 25% claim is not tested by the design. The experiment can only support a weaker claim about classification performance improvement over SPC.

On preprocessing: the explicit statement in Section 3 is a principle violation.

On temporal sampling: within-machine autocorrelation is a legitimate concern. A proper design would use temporal holdout within each machine's history.

all_issues_adjudicated: Preprocessing transformers fit on entire dataset — stated leakage
Stratified random sampling on time-series creates within-machine temporal contamination
Hypothesis claims 25% downtime reduction but design measures only classification metrics — central claim unvalidated
